title: ABI, Shared Libraries, and Foreign-Function Memory
description: A C library shares opaque handles and buffers with Rust, Python, and WebAssembly consumers that use different allocators and unwind rules. Learners cumulatively produce versioned cross-language ownership ABI through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Characterize calling convention, symbol visibility, integer widths, alignment, layout, and compiler flags for each supported ABI.
Expose stable opaque handles with versioned constructors, operations, observers, and same-library destruction.
Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.
